nts1.d: Part of the NTS family since day dot, Moxie has held her Wednesday show since the stations first broadcasts. Inviting esteemed guests for interviews & mixes and breaking new, unreleased music, she’s got your Wednesday afternoons covered… Tune in to hear anything from Soulful House, Garage, Afro beats and all the way through to rolling techno.
After work fun and global tunes with Tash LC and guests every other Wednesday

nts1.n: TOUCHING BASS W/ ERROL & ALEX RITA
nts1.d: Join Errol and the Touching Bass family every month for regular voyages into inner-city, concrete jungle productions plus astral-facing funk, jazz, broken beat and anything else with a soulful pulse.
NTS's own Giulia Tess pulls us leftfield once a month.
Special guest shows from around the world.

nts1.n: Turmeric w/ The Twins & Human Reaction
nts1.d: Fraternal party starters The Twins takeover for regular trips into electroclash, experimental electronics, and Desi sounds, inviting on boundary pushing collaborators and friends.

Croatian DIY label Low Income $quad showcase their catalogue and extended family of producers - Expect neo trance sounds, reworked Memphis rap acapellas, 90s-inspired fast techno and noisy experimentalism.
From the host: "The best of Soundcloud"

nts1.n: CDMX TAPES W/ LUXIXI & DJ CO.KR (R)
nts1.d:
From the creative mind of Cruel Santino, Subaru Live Radio on NTS explores Nigeria's alternative music scene with rare sounds, global collaborations, exclusive cuts, and special guest features. Set in an immersive underwater theme, each episode brings listeners deeper into Subaru World, building a lasting library of groundbreaking music for future generations to come, soundscaped with ads and various infomercials. 
Visually, Volume 2 expands, capturing raw Subaru-like visuals directed and edited by Santi for upcoming artists while also profiling their background and influences in gaming.

Rail Up resident DJ and visual artist Kelman Duran broadcasts monthly with original edits and guests.
From the host: "MÂNAA invites to create spaces of freedom and culture out of the ordinary structures while creating a platform for sharing and exchange. Through memory and history she likes to tell her story with the rhythm of percussions."

nts1.n: Women of Video Game Music: Yuka Kitamura (R)
nts1.d: As lead composer at FromSoftware, the Japanese developer deeply beloved by hardcore gamers across the world, Yuka Kitamura has soundtracked the virtual deaths of millions of players. Her music, which soundtrack the tough as nails, worlds of Dark Souls, Elden Ring, and Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice, is as tense and foreboding as the worlds themselves.
Visual artist and founder and editor-in-chief of The Editorial Magazine Claire Milbrath joins us for an hour of ambient, classical, and italian library music.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ PAM
THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO & NASHPAINTS (LIVE)
bail3 da th4ys w/ rafa duartt
CHANNELING W/ IVAN SMAGGHE & NATHAN GREGORY WILKINS (R)

nts2.d: 1-hour monthly show with composer and musician Astrid Sonne. Sharing everything from rock to dub to classical to pop.

Enter the mythology of NYC artist James K - her sound, equally organic and electronic, is a combination of odd dreams, hybridizing enchanted samples, ripped, broken and morphed rhythms, and vocals, both incomprehensible and appealing.
From the host: "Trip Lick this month features a takeover from Wonja!"

nts2.n: BLUE SCREEN w/ AMAR: Chris Cunningham Poetics
nts2.d:
Hosted by @amarediriwira, Blue Screen is a show experimenting with the concept of ‘audio screenings’, presenting extracts of films and visual artefacts along with commentary or within mixtapes.
From the host: "A one-hour transmission through the work of Chris Cunningham — moving from Warp-era body horror to late-90s corporate futurism."

nts2.n: THE WINDMILLS OF YOUR MIND w/ Taylor Rowley
nts2.d: DJ & music supervisor Taylor Rowley forages for the forgotten musical past every month, uncovering soundtracks, psych rock & other treasures.

nts2.n: SOLID STATE SURVIVOR W/ SHAGS CHAMBERLAIN
nts2.d:
Broadcasting from LA, Shags dives deep into his wild array of records, bringing a strong thematic focus each month.
From the host: "Over Twelve years into an illustrious career as The Drummer From Santana - Michael Shrieve, took a surprising side quest into the world of Electronic Music in his experimental collaborations with Klaus Schultze. Here is a bit of a patchwork of some of my favs from those albums."
Modern melancholia, waves from yesterdays, teenage rebellion and electronic electronics.
The inimitable Kranky Ltd, live from LA once a month.

nts2.n: Music To Relax w/ Mydreamfever - 5. In the Same Light
nts2.d:
Dreamlike shoegaze and distorted confessionals from Seoul’s anonymous underground wunderkind.
From the host: "On February 24, I traveled to Tokyo for a performance. While recording the cabin ambience I fell asleep. This album gathers the haze of that moment, the texture of my drifting sleep, and the longing of interacting with strangers in a world that can no longer return to what it was before."

Nipaluna ambient-pop duo Troth share esoteric and radiant sounds from the bottom of the earth. Bandcamp / Substack
From the host: "An audio diary, featuring bootleg live recordings of artists who joined us on a February 2026 Japan tour, improv live jams with Takeshi Ueno and Satoru Ono, phone field recordings and rounded out with studio recordings of friends made during the trip."

60 minutes of music from Pakistani singer Nahid Akhtar, dubbed the "Nightingale of Pakistan". Akhtar is most famous for her work as a Lollywood "playback singer", performing vocals that would be dubbed into Pakistani musicals. 
Selected by Paradise Bangkok's Chris Menist.

nts2.n: THE HEAD ZONE W/ RIPLEY JOHNSON
nts2.d: Step into The Head Zone - one hour of musical selections from the mind of Moon Duo's Ripley Johnson.

nts2.n: No ID Radio w/ Anysia Kym (R)
Mountain Cry w/ Marylou & Ziad Naoufal - Ruptured Label Special
AUTOTUNE THE WORLD W/ DJ Cuica
Prison Divorce Bombshell w/ Karly Hartzman

subtle.d:
DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

subtle.n: Pattern Recognition w/ R A L L Y
Mid Morning Matters w/ Alfie Danger
Taking the Peel w/ Badly Drawn Banana

subtle.d:
DJ and radio producer TILDA will be live from the studio to kick off your weekend. She’ll cover all corners of electronic music as well as showcasing unmissable new tracks & chatting about motives for the weekend. 

Each month TILDA will be closing the show with a signature mix.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Following his widely acclaimed breakout moment winning the 2013 DMC World Online Championships, Jon1st quickly built an international reputation as a ‘DJ’s DJ’. Not only proving himself as one of the world’s most diverse and musical turntablists, Jon also established himself as an on the pulse club DJ mixing across the alternative bass music spectrum and as an in demand live performer around the world, as well as recording acclaimed guest mixes for everyone from DJ Mag to Boiler Room to Ninja Tune’s Solid Steel to Mary Anne Hobbs to Keep Hush.

subtle.n: 5 Decades of Dub w/ Joe Vincent

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

dublab.n: In Sheep's Clothing – World of Sound
Dustin Wong – Why Did Why Did Why Did

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.

dublab.n: Dor Wand – Open Heart Social Club
Andy Oro and Akumen - Made to Move
McAllister and Kyle Art Threat - Please and Thank You
Mike Harding and Bana Haffar - Long Wave
Echo Park Film Center - Optical Track
The Urxed - Seeing It As You Really Are

rinse.n: DJ Rashad Special with Kode9, Scratcha DVA, Ashes57, Fauzia & more

rinse.j: LA CHISMOTEKA ft. Orfigyal >>> PERRAS CORE
